title: Small Reduction In Global Grain Trade Forecast
section_path: Small Reduction In Global Grain Trade Forecast
section_path_text: Small Reduction In Global Grain Trade Forecast
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) recently released their second global grain export forecast for the upcoming 2022/23 season. The forecast is still very preliminary, but of note is that only 486.3 million tons of exports are expected. This is 1 million tons less than was forecast a month ago and would mark a year-on-year decline of 11.9 million tons (-2%) from the 498.2 million tons that is now expected for the current 2021/22 season.
